Solar

Working Model · Batch 2021–2024

A fully fabricated solar-powered electric buggy built from scratch — raw steel to working prototype. Every component designed, welded, wired, and tested in the college workshop.

Engineering Problem Solving

Challenges & Fixes

⚠ Problem
Insufficient power output from the initial single solar panel — not enough wattage to charge the battery and power the motor simultaneously.
✓ Solution
Added a second 100W monocrystalline panel increasing total output to 200W. Fabricated a custom extended roof frame to mount both panels at optimal tilt angle.
⚠ Problem
DC brushless motor overheating during extended operation, causing performance drop and risk of damage to motor windings.
✓ Solution
Installed an aluminium heat sink on the motor casing and redesigned the enclosure to improve airflow circulation around the motor compartment.
